blocks.content:
-rw-rw-r--+ 35 3646 1040 10M Apr 18 23:06 articles/2026/01/28/articles.jsonl
3398 articles/2026/01/28/articles.jsonl
- IAEA/Ukraine: no direct meeting-request report, but the substations/nuclear-safety story continues to point more toward Ukraine requesting a special watchdog meeting than Russia. Raised Ukraine slightly.
- Sudan: Jan. 24 had no direct siege-broken report. Kept North Darfur live but moved North Kordofan level with it because Reuters-style reporting has emphasized al-Obeid and Kordofan pressure.
- Greenland/tariffs: continued Greenland/Denmark coverage supports Denmark as the target country, even though the immediate crisis may be moving toward a framework deal.
- New Jan. 24 questions: for the Dnipropetrovsk miners-bus attack, kept Pavlohrad first but raised Marganets as a serious mining-town alternative; for the southern Lebanon abduction statement, raised Hezbollah and added Amal Movement as a small hedge.

Watched but no update:
- Indigenous-rights explosive-device city, Greek factory-explosion city, Bisan Owda platform ban, foreign leader Australia-visit threats, Pat Cummins replacement, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Champions League draw and Crans-Montana skier crash had no Jan. 24 naming signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-25 Update

Resolution events:
- Indigenous-rights explosive-device city resolved to Perth. I had Perth only as a small tail. Lesson: for Australia Day protest-security incidents, include Perth materially; large rallies are not only Melbourne/Sydney/Canberra.
- Sudan siege-broken state resolved to South Kordofan. I had South Kordofan live but behind North Darfur/North Kordofan. Lesson: when reporting mentions Nuba Mountains/South Kordofan under siege, do not let al-Obeid/North Kordofan coverage dominate the distribution.
- Tariff country resolved to South Korea. I missed entirely by overfitting the Greenland/Denmark tariff story. Lesson: tariff-percentage questions can be trade-policy/Asia stories even when contemporaneous geopolitics dominates headlines; include South Korea/Japan/China when the clue is a 15-to-25 percent import-tariff increase.
- Gaza child evacuation destination resolved to Jordan. I had Jordan as a tail behind Italy/Egypt. Lesson: WHO medical evacuations from Gaza often go to Jordan; keep Jordan near the lead when exact transfer counts are asked.
- Greek factory-explosion city resolved to Trikala. I missed by only using major/industrial-city priors. Lesson: Greek accident city markets can resolve to mid-sized regional cities; include Trikala/Ioannina/Lamia-style locations when there is no direct signal.

Updates made:
- Super Bowl: New England beat Denver 10-7 in the AFC title game, so the market is now Patriots vs Seahawks only. Moved New England narrowly ahead of Seattle, while keeping it close because the AFC game was ugly and weather-skewed.
- Australian Open men: Alcaraz beat Tommy Paul in straight sets and remains setless; Djokovic received a walkover into the quarters, improving his rest/fitness profile. Raised Alcaraz and Djokovic, lowered Sinner after the prior cramp/heat issue pending his next match.
- South Sudan displacement: Jan. 25 article headline says fresh conflict displaced 180,000, but full content was unavailable. Raised Upper Nile substantially because recent South Sudan displacement/conflict coverage most often points there, with Jonglei/Unity/Warrap/Western Equatoria tails.
- Gaza mission troops: Pakistan formally agreed to the Board of Peace/ISF and was reported willing to contribute 3,500 troops, so raised Pakistan behind Indonesia while keeping Indonesia first because the question's phrase is up to 8,000 troops.

Watched but no update:
- IAEA special meeting requester, Fury comeback opponent, Bisan Owda platform ban, foreign leader Australia-visit threats, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Pat Cummins replacement, Ukrainian miners-bus attack, Lebanese abduction group, Champions League draw and Crans-Montana skier crash had no Jan. 25 signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-26 Update

Resolution events:
- IAEA special Ukraine meeting requester resolved to Netherlands. I missed by assigning most mass to Ukraine/Russia. Lesson: for IAEA or UN watchdog procedural requests, include small/mid-sized European states and Dutch diplomats when they are quoted; the requester need not be a direct conflict party.

Updates made:
- Tyson Fury comeback opponent: Jan. 26 Sky Sports item named Agron Smakici as putting himself forward for Fury's April comeback fight and tied him to the sparring-cut drama. Moved Smakici ahead of Makhmudov, while keeping Makhmudov live because Fury's manager previously called him a top-three option.
- South Sudan displacement state: Jan. 26 reporting said the military ordered evacuations from parts of Jonglei and that some of the fiercest fighting is there. Moved Jonglei ahead of Upper Nile for the UN displacement figure.
- Carrick/Manchester United first defeat: United beat Arsenal after already beating Manchester City under Carrick. Removed those stale leaders and moved to Tottenham, Liverpool, West Ham, Fulham and Chelsea.
- Super Bowl: opening odds made Seattle a clear favorite over New England, with Seahawks roughly -200 to -235 and a 3.5- to 4.5-point spread. Moved Seattle to 66%.
- Australian Open men: Sinner recovered from the cramp scare and beat Darderi in straight sets with strong serving, narrowing the gap to Alcaraz. Kept Alcaraz barely first, Sinner close second, Djokovic third.
- Grammy Album of the Year: AP preview split between Lady Gaga's "Mayhem" narrative and Kendrick Lamar's "GNX" cultural footprint; resolution criteria ask for artist names, so corrected the forecast from album-title strings to Lady Gaga/Kendrick Lamar/Bad Bunny etc.

Watched but no update:
- Bisan Owda platform ban, foreign leader Australia-visit threats, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Pat Cummins replacement, Ukrainian miners-bus attack, Balochistan secured town, Champions League playoff draw, Crans-Montana skier crash, Gaza mission troops and southern Lebanon abduction group had no Jan. 26 naming signal strong enough for a confident move.

## 2026-01-27 Update

Resolution events:
- Fury comeback opponent resolved to Arslanbek Makhmudov. The Jan. 26 Smakici story was a useful late signal but less authoritative than the earlier manager quote calling Makhmudov a top-three option. Lesson: for official opponent announcements, keep manager/promoter shortlist comments ahead of fighter self-promotion.
- Australia online-threats visit resolved to Isaac Herzog. I missed by focusing on Netanyahu/Modi. Lesson: for Australian planned-visit threat cases, include Israeli president/head-of-state visits, not only prime ministers and Indian leaders.
- Bisan Owda platform ban resolved to TikTok. I missed by overweighting Meta. Lesson: current U.S./Trump-era platform-control and censorship stories can point to TikTok even for Palestinian-journalist account bans.

Updates made:
- Australian Open men: Alcaraz beat De Minaur 7-5, 6-2, 6-1 and reached his first Melbourne semifinal; Zverev also beat Learner Tien. Removed De Minaur, raised Alcaraz, kept Sinner second ahead of his Shelton quarter, and raised Djokovic/Zverev modestly.
- Super Bowl: Jan. 27 reports said Drake Maye may have picked up a right-shoulder issue in the AFC title game, while Seattle remained the clear betting favorite. Raised Seattle to 70%.

Watched but no update:
- South Sudan displacement state, Netanyahu visitor, West African party dissolution, Champions League playoff-draw questions, Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Crans-Montana skier crash, Pat Cummins replacement, Ukrainian miners-bus attack, Balochistan secured town, market-fire city, Grammy Album of the Year, Libya killing, strategic-minerals stockpile and English League Cup final had no Jan. 27 signal strong enough to move.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd
m=pd.read_csv('market.csv', dtype={'qid':str})
print('rows',len(m),'active',(~m.is_resolved).sum(),'resolved',m.is_resolved.sum(),'no_pred_active',((~m.is_resolved)&(m.num_predictions==0)).sum())
active=m[~m.is_resolved].copy(); active['rd']=pd.to_datetime(active.resolution_date, errors='coerce')
for _,r in active.sort_values('rd').head(55).iterrows():
    print('\\n',r.qid, r.rd.date() if pd.notna(r.rd) else None, 'preds', r.num_predictions)
    print(r.title[:270])
    print('agg', r.market_aggregate)
PY"

events.preview: Jan. 28 resolved three more: Jonglei was correct after the Jan. 26 move, while Netanyahu’s visitor was Narendra Modi and the party-dissolution country was Burkina Faso. The highest-value remaining cluster is now the Jan.

blocks.text: Jan. 28 resolved three more: Jonglei was correct after the Jan. 26 move, while Netanyahu’s visitor was Narendra Modi and the party-dissolution country was Burkina Faso. The highest-value remaining cluster is now the Jan. 29 Champions League playoff questions, so I’m scanning today’s match/result coverage first.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,textwrap
from pathlib import Path
patterns=[
 ('ucl_results_draw', ['Champions League','playoff']),
 ('ucl_real_madrid', ['Real Madrid','Benfica']),
 ('ucl_newcastle', ['Newcastle','PSG']),
 ('ucl_juventus', ['Juventus','Monaco']),
 ('ucl_mancity', ['Manchester City','Galatasaray']),
 ('ucl_table', ['Champions League','top eight']),
 ('epstein_commerce', ['Epstein','Commerce Secretary']),
 ('epstein_lutnick', ['Epstein','Lutnick']),
 ('epstein_ross', ['Epstein','Wilbur Ross']),
 ('crans_airlifted', ['Crans-Montana','airlifted']),
 ('crans_vonn', ['Crans-Montana','Vonn']),
 ('crans_macuga', ['Crans-Montana','Macuga']),
 ('cummins_replacement', ['Pat Cummins','replacement']),
 ('cummins_abbott', ['Cummins','Sean Abbott']),
 ('cummins_ellis', ['Cummins','Nathan Ellis']),
 ('ao_sinner', ['Australian Open','Sinner','Shelton']),
 ('ao_djokovic', ['Australian Open','Djokovic','Musetti']),
 ('ao_semifinal', ['Australian Open','semi-final']),
 ('grammy_aoty', ['Album of the Year','Grammy']),
 ('miners_bus', ['bus','miners','Dnipropetrovsk']),
 ('miners_pavlohrad', ['Pavlohrad','miners']),
 ('balochistan_battle', ['Balochistan','three-day battle']),
 ('balochistan_secured', ['Balochistan','secured','police']),
 ('market_fire', ['huge fire','market']),
 ('dhaka_fire', ['Dhaka','market','fire']),
 ('lagos_fire', ['Lagos','market','fire']),
 ('norway_corruption', ['Norwegian','prime minister','corruption']),
 ('strategic_minerals', ['strategic minerals','stockpile']),
 ('league_cup_final', ['League Cup final','Manchester City']),
 ('libya_haddad', ['Libya','Haddad','killed']),
 ('super_bowl', ['Super Bowl','Seahawks','Patriots']),
]
res=[]
with Path('articles/2026/01/28/articles.jsonl').open() as f:
  for i,line in enumerate(f,1):
    try:a=json.loads(line)
    except: continue
    text=(a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or '')
    low=text.lower()
    for label,terms in patterns:
      if all(t.lower() in low for t in terms):
        pos=min([low.find(t.lower()) for t in terms if low.find(t.lower())>=0] or [0])
        snip=text[max(0,pos-300):pos+1100].replace('\\n',' ')
        res.append((label,i,a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),a.get('title'),snip))
        break
from collections import defaultdict
counts=defaultdict(int)
print('matches',len(res))
for label,i,date,src,title,snip in res:
  if counts[label]>=8: continue
  counts[label]+=1
  print(f\"\\n## {label} line {i} pub={date} src={src}\\nTITLE: {title}\\nSNIP: {textwrap.shorten(snip, width=1300, placeholder=' ...')}\")
print('\\ncounts')
for k in sorted(counts): print(k, counts[k])
PY"

## ao_sinner line 550 pub=2026-01-28 src=www.irishtimes.com
TITLE: Jannik Sinner set for ‘toughest challenge’ in semi-final against Novak Djokovic
SNIP: Jannik Sinner set for ‘toughest challenge’ in semi-final against Novak Djokovic Jannik Sinner says his semi-final meeting with Novak Djokovic presents one of the toughest challenges in tennis after defeating Ben Shelton in the Australian Open quarter-finals on Wednesday. “It’s very difficult,” Sinner said. “It’s one of the toughest challenges we have in our sport. It’s great to have Novak playing at this very, very high level. It’s a Grand Slam, it’s always very difficult against Novak. Let’s see what’s coming.” Sinner’s 6-3, 6-4, 6-4 quarter-final victory over the seventh seed extended his commanding record against Shelton to 9-1, with Sinner winning 22 consecutive sets since the 23-year-old American won their first meeting. Such dominance over one of the best young players in the world indicates just how far ahead Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz are of the rest of the field. Since a brief hiccup in his third-round match against Eliot Spizzirri, where he suffered from cramps in hot conditions, Sinner has not put a foot wrong. The second seed’s stratospheric level marks a stark contrast to Djokov

TITLE: Champions League live updates: All 36 teams play at same time in final league matches
SNIP: Champions League live updates: All 36 teams play at same time in final league matches Here are the permutations for tonight’s games, covered in this article: [ Champions League permutations: who needs what from final night of fixtures?Opens in new window ] Hello and welcome to live coverage of the final matches of the league phase of the Champions League. All 36 teams will be in action at the same time - 8pm - over 18 matches across Europe. The top 8 teams get to skip the a round and automatically get into the last 16, with teams 9th to 24th playing against each other in the playoff round. Teams 25th to 36th are eliminated. Arsenal and Bayern Munich are guaranteed to finish in the top 8, technically as far down as Qarabag in 18th could finish in the other six spots, while everyone as far down as Ajax in 32nd could finish in the top 24. Eintracht Frankfurt, Slavia Prague, Villarreal and Kairat have already been eliminated. Here are the matches on tonight: Ajax vs Olympiakos Arsenal vs Kairat Almaty Athletic Club vs Sporting CP Atletico Madrid vs Bodø/Glimt Barcelona vs FC Copenhagen B

TITLE: Elena Rybakina blows away Iga Swiatek to reach Australian Open semi-final
SNIP: Elena Rybakina blows away Iga Swiatek to reach Australian Open semi-final Elena Rybakina took a significant step towards her second Grand Slam title as she overpowered and outplayed the second seed Iga Swiatek in the quarter-finals of the Australian Open, advancing 7-5, 6-1 to end Swiatek’s hopes of completing the career Grand Slam this year. This immense victory sends Rybakina, the fifth seed and 2023 Australian Open finalist, into her fourth Grand Slam semi-final. It has been nearly four years since the 26-year-old made her first breakthrough by winning Wimbledon in 2022. Although she has won numerous big titles and established herself as one of the best players in the world, she has as yet failed to drag herself over the line at the Grand Slams for a second time. However, Rybakina arrived in Melbourne playing some of the best tennis of her career after dismantling the field at the WTA Finals last November. The disdain with which she put Swiatek away here in set two reinforced her status as one of the leading contenders among the final four. TITLE: Seahawks QB Sam Darnold called top Super Bowl human interest story
SNIP: Seahawks QB Sam Darnold called top Super Bowl human interest story NEWYou can now listen to Fox News articles! The Super Bowl averaged 127.7 million viewers on FOX last year, ranging from die-hard NFL fans, to the casuals hanging out at a party enjoying the "Big Game." While some know the X's and O's more than others, there are usually many human interest stories leading up to kickoff, and "NFL RedZone" host Scott Hanson already knows the greatest one of all in the matchup between the New England Patriots and Seattle Seahawks. "Sam Darnold is that story," Hanson told Fox News Digital about the Seahawks' starting quarterback. CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM The human interest isn't always the starting quarterback of one of the teams playing, but given Darnold's journey to get to this point in his career, Hanson feels anyone with "no horse in the race" has to root for him. "Sam Darnold, cast aside, dismissed. Every NFL player has felt that at some point in their life," Hanson said, while also discussing while also discussing his excitement for Lowe's "Earn Your Sunda

## super_bowl line 866 pub=2026-01-28 src=www.foxnews.com
TITLE: Patriots: Julian Edelman reveals Bill Belichick's most memorable rule
SNIP: Patriots: Julian Edelman reveals Bill Belichick's most memorable rule NEWYou can now listen to Fox News articles! At the height of the New England Patriots dynasty, Bill Belichick had a famous docket of rules. Super Bowl LI MVP Julian Edelman remembers Belichick's most "memorable" rule. "The most memorable rule I always remember him saying is nothing could, nothing burning can be in your hotel rooms," Edelman told Fox News Digital in August, while promoting the sleeping pill Unisom. "'I don't' care if it's incense, this scents, sage, if it burns you can't have it.'" ═══ [1] ═══════════════════════════════════════
HEADLINE: When is Champions League draw? Date, time, teams qualified and how to watch
SOURCE: www.independent.co.uk
PUBLISHED: 2026-01-28 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-01-28 00:00:00
URL: https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/football/champions-league-draw-date-time-teams-tv-knockout-playoff-phase-b2909786.html

When is Champions League draw? Date, time, teams qualified and how to watch

The League Phase of the 2025-26 Champions League has drawn to a close after a dramatic league-phase finale. The next 16 teams will enter the knockout phase’s play-off draw, with the top eight sides permitted a bye into the last 16. That valuable rest, in a packed schedule, could prove decisive in the hunt for silverware. Though Paris Saint-Germain proved it is not obstacle to winning Europe’s most coveted club trophy, dismantling Brest in the play-offs before a famous run to last year’s final and a thumping win over Inter Milan to clinch European glory. Here’s everything we know about the Champions League knockout stage’s play-off draw:
The 2025/26 UEFA Champions League knockout phase play-off draw starts at 11am GMT on Friday, 30 January 2026, in Nyon, Switzerland. The draw includes teams that finished ninth to 24th place in the league phase, with the top eight sides in the league phase qualifying automatically for the last 16. There are 16 teams in the Champions League knockout phase play-off draw, including defending champions PSG - who were forced to go through this stage on the path to glory last years - as well as record competition winners Real Madrid. With the number representing their finish in the league phase, the following teams are seeded:
9. Real Madrid (Spain)
10. Inter Milan (Italy)
11. Paris St. Germain (France)
12. Newcastle United (England)
13. Juventus (Italy)
14. Atletico Madrid (Spain)
15. Atalanta (Italy)
16. Bayer Leverkusen (Germany)
The remaining teams are unseeded:
17. Borussia Dortmund (Germany)
18. Olympiacos (Greece)
19. Club Brugge (Belgium)
20. Galatasaray (Turkey)
21. AS Monaco (France)
22. Qarabag (Azerbaijan)
23. Bodo/Glimt (Norway)
24. Benfica (Portugal)
The draw will be streamed live on Uefa’s website and the Champions League app, while Independent Sport will have a live blog with all the results from the draw and the latest reaction.

blocks.content:
line 1457 www.independent.co.uk 2026-01-28 When is Champions League draw? Date, time, teams qualified and how to watch
The League Phase of the 2025-26 Champions League draws to a close tonight as fans wait to learn their team‘s route to
the final. The next 16 teams will enter the knockout phase’s play-off draw, with the top eight sides permitted a bye
into the last 16. That valuable rest, in a packed schedule, could prove decisive in the hunt for silverware. Though
Paris Saint-Germain proved it is not obstacle to winning Europe’s most coveted club trophy, dismantling Brest in the
play-offs before a famous run to last year’s final and a thumping win over Inter Milan to clinch European glory. Here’s
everything we know about the Champions League knockout stage’s play-off draw: The 2025/26 UEFA Champions League knockout
phase play-off draw starts at 11am GMT on Friday, 30 January 2026, in Nyon, Switzerland. The draw includes teams that
finished ninth to 24th place in the league phase, with the top eight sides in the league phase qualifying automatically
for the last 16. There will be 16 teams, to be decided on Wednesday, 28 January 2026 after the final round of fixtures.
Arsenal are guaranteed to finish first or second, while Bayern Munich are also into the last 16. All other teams, except
Eintracht Frankfurt, Slavia Prague, Villarreal, Kairat Almaty, who have all been eliminated, can qualify for the top
eight or advance to the play-off draw. Liverpool (fourth), Tottenham (fifth), Newcastle (seventh) and Chelsea (eighth)
are all currently holding an automatic route to the last 16, while Man City (11th) must rely on results elsewhere to
jump into the top eight and avoid the play-offs. Other teams hoping to move into the top eight after the final round
include Barcelona, Atletico Madrid, Inter Milan, Juventus and Borussia Dortmund. The draw will be streamed live on
Uefa’s website and the Champions League app, while Independent Sport will have a live blog with all the results from the
draw and the latest reaction. The clubs are paired together in relation to their league phase positions, forming four
seeded pairs: positions 9 and 10, 11 and 12, 13 and 14, and 15 and 16. There will be four unseeded pairs: positions 17
and 18, 19 and 20, 21 and 22, and 23 and 24). Clubs in each seeded pair will draw a tie against an unseeded pair: clubs
9 or 10 against clubs 23 or 24, clubs 11 or 12 against clubs 21 or 22, clubs 13 or 14 against clubs 19 or 20, and clubs
15 or 16 against clubs 17 or 18. Teams from the same national association, or teams that have already met in the league
phase, can all play against each other in the knockout phase play-offs. Matches will take place over two legs, with the
seeded team set to play the return leg at home. First legs will be played on 17-18 February and second legs are slated
to take place on 24-25 February. When is the round of 16 draw? The eight teams that advance from the knockout phase
play-offs will be in the draw for the round of 16, which will take place on Friday, 27 February.

### 85609 Which club will Real Madrid be drawn to face in the UEFA Champions League playoff round by January 30, 2026? criteria
  • Source of Truth: UEFA's official Champions League draw results, bracket, or fixture list published on UEFA.com will be used.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when UEFA publicly confirms Real Madrid's opponent for the playoff round, expected on or about January 30, 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the opposing club name only, exactly or near-exactly as listed by UEFA.
background Question Start Date: 2026-01-14. The UEFA Champions League is using a league phase followed by a playoff round for clubs that do not go straight to the round of 16. A draw later in January is set to determine several knockout matchups. ### 55999 Which club will Benfica face in the first leg of its Champions League playoff on February 17, 2026? criteria
  • Source of Truth: This question will resolve using the official UEFA fixture list, match centre, or match report for Benfica's playoff first leg scheduled for February 17, 2026. Reputable sports coverage may be used only as backup confirmation.
  • Resolution Date: The question resolves on February 17, 2026, when Benfica's opponent for that first-leg match is officially listed.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the opponent club's commonly used official short name, exactly or substantially matching the official match record.
background Question Start Date: 2026-01-28. UEFA's men's club season continues in February with knockout playoff ties. Benfica is scheduled to play the opening match of a two-leg playoff tie in mid-February. ### 261538 In which city will Newcastle United play the first leg of its 2025-26 UEFA Champions League playoff tie in February 2026? criteria
  • Source of Truth: The official UEFA fixture schedule on UEFA.com. Newcastle United's official match schedule may be used as a secondary confirming source if necessary.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when UEFA publishes the first-leg playoff fixture details, expected by February 1, 2026, and no later than the kickoff of Newcastle United's first playoff match in February 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The city name only, in English, for the location hosting Newcastle United's first-leg playoff match.
background Question Start Date: 2026-01-19. UEFA Champions League playoff ties are played over two legs. After the draw, UEFA publishes the fixture list, including which team hosts the first leg and the city where that match is played. ### 35131 Which club will eliminate Juventus in the 2025/26 UEFA Champions League playoff round and reach the last 16 by February 2026? criteria
  • UEFA's official match reports, aggregate playoff results, and confirmed list of round-of-16 qualifiers on UEFA.com will be used.
  • This question resolves when the Juventus playoff tie is complete and UEFA confirms which club advances, expected by late February 2026 and no later than February 26, 2026.
  • Provide the official English club name of the team that wins Juventus's playoff tie and qualifies for the round of 16.
background Question Start Date: 2026-01-09. The Champions League playoff round is about to decide the remaining places in the round of 16. Juventus is one of the clubs trying to advance from this knockout stage.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
s=json.loads(Path('../state.json').read_text())
print('current_date',s.get('current_date'))
print('total_predictions',s.get('total_predictions'))
events=s.get('resolution_events') or []
print('resolution events',len(events))
for ev in events[-12:]: print(ev)
PY"

/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
s=json.loads(Path('../state.json').read_text())
print('current_date',s.get('current_date'))
print('total_predictions',s.get('total_predictions'))
events=s.get('resolution_events') or []
print('resolution events',len(events))
